Project: DuckDice Casino
Type: Open-source provably fair crypto dice casino

Goal:
Create the best dice betting interface with extremely fast UX,
provably fair transparency, bot-friendly APIs and DeFi bankroll support.

Tech Stack

Frontend
- Next.js
- React
- TypeScript
- TailwindCSS
- WebSocket client

Backend
- Go or Rust services
- REST API
- WebSocket event streaming

Smart Contracts
- Solidity
- Hardhat
- Ethereum-compatible chain

Infrastructure
- Docker
- Redis
- PostgreSQL

Key UX Goals

1. The entire betting flow must be usable in under 2 seconds.
2. The betting controls must always be visible without scrolling.
3. The dice roll result must feel immediate and responsive.
4. The UI must show provably fair information clearly.
5. The interface must support both human players and bot users.

Interface Layout

Header
Logo
Wallet balance
LP share
DAO governance link

Main layout uses two columns.

Left column (Dice Controls)
- Bet amount input
- Chance slider
- Roll over / under toggle
- Quick bet buttons (½, 2×, max)
- Autobet toggle
- Roll button

Right column (Statistics)
- Last 20 rolls
- Profit/Loss graph
- Bet distribution
- Leaderboard
- Bot activity panel

Center
Animated dice result display.

Bottom
Chat
Strategy panel
Bot configuration.

Footer
Provably fair seed verification
Terms
Documentation

Dice Game Rules

Dice produces values between 0.00 and 99.99.

RNG must be provably fair using HMAC-SHA256:

roll = HMAC_SHA256(server_seed, client_seed + nonce)

Output must be deterministic and verifiable.

Chance Slider Behaviour

When user adjusts the chance slider:

update
- win probability
- payout multiplier
- win threshold

All updates must happen instantly without server calls.

Dice Animation

Dice animation should last between 300ms and 700ms.

The animation must visually roll before revealing the deterministic result.

Winning rolls show gold particle effects.
Losing rolls show subtle fade.

Performance Rules

UI must stay responsive during rapid betting.

Support:

20+ bets per second
sub-100ms response times

Do not block UI rendering.

Autobet System

Autobet panel must allow:

bet count
profit target
loss limit
bet interval
optional random delay

Autobet must display live statistics.

Bot Friendly Design

The same logic used by the UI must be accessible via API.

API endpoints

POST /bet
GET /rolls
GET /bankroll
GET /stats

WebSocket events

bet_placed
bet_result
bankroll_update

Provably Fair UI

The interface must display

server seed hash
client seed
nonce
verification link

Players must be able to verify previous rolls.

Accessibility

Use strong color contrast.

Never rely on color alone to show win/loss.

Support keyboard navigation.

Mobile UX

The dice animation must remain centered.

Controls must be reachable with thumbs.

Chat and statistics panels must be collapsible.

Design Language

Primary colors

deep purple
neon cyan

Win color

gold

Loss color

red fade

Animations must feel smooth and modern but not distracting.

Code Style

Use modular React components.

Example components

DiceControls
ChanceSlider
BetInput
DiceAnimation
RollHistory
AutobetPanel
Leaderboard
ProvablyFairPanel

All components must be reusable and isolated.

State Management

Use React hooks.

Avoid unnecessary global state.

Performance Optimizations

Use memoization for expensive calculations.

Avoid unnecessary re-renders.

WebSocket updates should update only relevant UI components.
